security_philosophy_public_statements.summary: In a CoreWeave 'AI Cloud Essentials' episode, James Higgins described how AI fundamentally alters the shared responsibility model, stating that responsibility 'between data and models and model integrity are now shared between the customer and the provider.' He emphasized the importance of cryptographic attestation at the Data Processing Unit (DPU) layer to ensure infrastructure integrity, calling it a way to 'ensure with... cryptographically signed attestation that what is running underneath the workloads... is exactly what we intended to be running.' He also highlighted that 'AI governance is going to be the key attribute' and stressed the need to 'do continuous auditing' and 'invest in AI native infrastructure' to securely support regulated and enterprise workloads.

security_philosophy_key_priorities: AI governance and continuous auditing of models/data flows
Shift from perimeter-only defenses to data/model integrity with cryptographic attestation (DPU edge)
Cloud threat detection and automation in detection and response (D&R)
Clarifying and operationalizing the cloud shared responsibility model
Investing in AI-native infrastructure for regulated/enterprise workloads
output.security_philosophy_frameworks_and_stand: James Higgins publicly supports and is associated with several key security frameworks and concepts. He played a foundational role in the Linux Foundation's Open Source Security Foundation (OpenSSF), through which he advocates for improving open-source supply chain security. He has also discussed zero-trust principles, with resources from one of his podcast appearances referencing Google's BeyondCorp model. Additionally, he is a strong advocate for establishing and communicating clarity around the cloud shared responsibility model, particularly as it evolves with AI workloads.

output.current_company_security_leadership_stra: As CISO, Jim Higgins is shaping CoreWeave's security strategy by integrating a 'security by design' approach into every layer of the platform, a strategy publicly endorsed by the company. This involves a multi-faceted technical foundation that includes hardware isolation, robust identity federation using standards like OIDC, SAML, and SCIM, single-tenant node isolation for Kubernetes workloads, comprehensive encryption for data in transit and at rest, and DPU-accelerated networking with NVIDIA BlueField-3. Building on this foundation, Higgins' public statements emphasize a forward-looking strategy focused on the unique challenges of AI. He advocates for maturing AI-native governance and implementing continuous auditing processes for models and data. A key pillar of his strategy is leveraging cryptographic attestation at the Data Processing Unit (DPU) level to ensure infrastructure integrity. Furthermore, he is focused on clearly defining and operationalizing a shared responsibility model tailored to the complexities of AI cloud services, ensuring customers understand their role in securing their data and models.

current_company_compliance_certification.access_method: Detailed audit reports and attestations are available under a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) via the CoreWeave Trust Center (trust.coreweave.com) or directly from the account team.

current_company_technical_architecture_d: Identity and Access Management (IAM): The platform features a native IAM system that supports integration with enterprise Identity Providers (IdPs) through protocols such as SCIM for automated user provisioning, as well as OIDC and SAML for federation.

Kubernetes and Container Security: The CoreWeave Kubernetes Service (CKS) is designed with robust node isolation, providing each customer with single-tenant nodes to ensure maximum workload and resource isolation.

Network Security: CoreWeave utilizes NVIDIA BlueField-3 DPUs to power virtual, accelerated networks. It enforces network privacy through Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) isolation, ensuring customer traffic remains segregated. All network traffic is encrypted.

Data Encryption: The platform implements encryption for data at rest across its storage solutions and ensures data is encrypted in transit, including for its AI Object Storage. Access is further controlled by IAM and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) policies.

Vulnerability and Threat Management: CoreWeave maintains a proactive security posture by continuously monitoring for and patching common vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs). The company also engages industry-leading third-party firms to conduct regular penetration tests of its platform.

Physical Security: Data centers are protected by a multi-layered physical security model, including biometric access controls, 24/7 CCTV monitoring, and strictly limited access for authorized personnel.

current_company_security_partnerships.partnership_scope: A strategic global partnership to power the secure AI cloud. The collaboration integrates CoreWeave's high-performance AI Cloud infrastructure with the CrowdStrike Falcon platform to provide industry-leading protection for AI workloads running in production environments.

output.current_company_shared_responsibility_mo: CoreWeave publicly documents a Shared Responsibility Model that clearly delineates security and compliance duties between itself and its customers. According to the model, client responsibilities include securing customer data, managing application-level security, defining IAM policies, configuring third-party Identity Providers (IdPs), managing network policies, and handling data classification, protection, disaster recovery, and backups. CoreWeave's responsibilities cover the underlying infrastructure, including the Platform Layer (Kubernetes, APIs, IAM platform, console, VPC), the Compute Layer (OS, drivers, health checks, EDR), the Network Layer (connectivity, encapsulation, VPC isolation, direct connect), and the Physical Layer (data center security, power, cooling, asset management). This model is accessible via the company's documentation and was last updated on January 27, 2026.

Excerpts announcing the CrowdStrike partnership explicitly connect CoreWeave’s platform to a leading security ecosystem, implying enhanced detection, response, and telemetry capabilities. Subsequent excerpts from CoreWeave Trust Center and security documentation emphasize platform security, defined shared responsibility, and formal security governance, which contextualize how threat detection and response duties are distributed and how security is embedded into the architecture. Additional excerpts detailing CoreWeave’s security features (IAM, network isolation, encryption, and monitoring/patching practices) further support the existence of mature security controls that would enable cloud threat detection and automated response workflows. Although none of the excerpts provide a verbatim statement of the exact phrasing of the requested field value, the combination of security partnerships, governance, and architectural controls strongly supports the presence of cloud threat detection and automation in D&R as part of CoreWeave’s security posture.

executive_summary.description: Brief strategic overview of who this person is, their role, and why they matter for outreach. 2-3 sentences max.

person_full_name.description: The person's verified full name, confirming or expanding on the input with formal name variants like 'James Higgins'.

person_current_title.description: The person's current, verified job title.

person_current_role_start_date.description: The verified start date (month and year) for the person's current role.

person_linkedin_url.description: The person's LinkedIn profile URL, verified as active and matching their current role.

person_total_experience.description: Approximate total years of experience in the security field or other relevant domains, based on career history.

title.description: Job title held at the company.

start_date.description: Start date of the role (Month Year).

end_date.description: End date of the role (Month Year) or 'Present'.

notable_details.description: Key achievements, scope, or context relevant to security or GRC.

career_history.description: A list of the person's previous roles in reverse chronological order. Each entry should include the company, title, start date, end date, and any notable details or achievements, particularly those relevant to security, GRC, or leadership.

source_title.description: The title of the podcast, interview, or article where the statement was made.

source_url.description: The URL to the public statement.

publication_date.description: The date the statement was published or recorded.

summary.description: A summary or key quote from the public statement.

security_philosophy_key_priorities.description: A list of the person's stated key security priorities, such as AI governance, AI-native infrastructure, cloud threat detection, and managing the shared responsibility model in AI.

security_philosophy_frameworks_and_stand.description: Any security frameworks, standards, or methodologies the person has publicly advocated for or been associated with, such as Open Source Security Foundation (OpenSSF).

organization.description: Name of the advisory board, industry group, or academic institution.

role.description: The person's role within the organization (e.g., Board Member, Senior Strategic Advisor).

start_date.description: The start date of the affiliation (Month Year).

description.description: A brief description of the person's involvement or the organization's focus.

advisory_and_affiliations.description: A list of the person's advisory board seats, industry group memberships, or academic affiliations. Each entry should specify the organization and their role (e.g., Senior Strategic Advisor, Board Member).

current_company_security_leadership_stra.description: An analysis of how this person is shaping the security strategy at their current company, based on their public statements and the company's security initiatives.

standard.description: The name of the compliance standard or certification (e.g., SOC 2, ISO 27001).

status.description: The current status of the certification (e.g., 'Aligned', 'Certified').

access_method.description: How compliance documentation can be accessed (e.g., 'Available upon request under NDA').

current_company_technical_architecture_d.description: A list of key details about the company's security architecture, such as hardware isolation, Kubernetes and container security, storage encryption, identity and access management (IAM), and network controls.

partner_name.description: The name of the security vendor or partner.

partnership_scope.description: A description of what the partnership covers (e.g., securing AI cloud infrastructure).

announcement_date.description: The date the partnership was publicly announced.

current_company_security_partnerships.description: A list of the company's security vendor partnerships. Each entry should include the partner's name and the scope of the partnership, such as the collaboration with CrowdStrike to secure AI cloud infrastructure.

current_company_shared_responsibility_mo.description: A description of how the company publicly defines the division of security responsibilities between itself as the provider and its customers.
